articles.title: RETAIL CATEGORY STRATEGIES FOR YOUTUBE
articles.summary: Retail content dominates YouTube. Fashion, home, beauty, and consumer electronics generate 688B views across 4.12M channels. Creators drive shopping behavior through reviews, tutorials, and short-form discovery. Viewers are 98% more likely to trust YouTube recommendations than other platforms. Fast-growing trends include practical problem-solving, shop-ertainment, and honest reviews.

articles.title: HOW BRANDS ARE AIMING TO BUILD ‘ALGORITHMIC TRUST' AHEAD OF AN AI AGENT-POWERED BLACK FRIDAY
articles.summary: Brands are working to build algorithmic trust so AI agents can surface their products during a longer, AI-driven shopping window. Most consumers have used AI tools at least once. 68% report trying tools like ChatGPT, but only 2% use AI to make purchases, which shows a large gap that brands want to close. New tools such as Pulse and Atlas from OpenAI may speed adoption by proactively presenting personalized deals to shoppers. Brands are responding by encoding product data for machine understanding, strengthening verifiable brand values, and improving online reputation so AI agents can confidently recommend their products.

articles.title: TURN INSULTS YOU GET INTO ADS
articles.summary: Reusing unfair insults can boost brand engagement and ad performance because it makes a brand seem more confident and humorous. Experiments showed that an electronics store increased its CTR by 26.7% when it embraced an insult, and MTV saw a 25% rise in interest, a 70.5% lift in humor, and a 20.2% increase in perceived confidence when it reused criticism. Vitamin Water saw similar gains, including a 67% rise in humor and a 21% rise in confidence. The approach fails when the critic is seen as vulnerable, as perceptions of a brand being a bully can rise by 59.9%.

articles.title: REDESIGN OF PROTON.AI'S CUSTOMER NEWSLETTER
articles.summary: This post shows how Proton's newsletter moved from plain text to a sports-themed design. It introduced a mascot, used ChatGPT to build the HTML layout, and tested the code in Parcel to confirm it rendered well. The prompt used is shared in the comments.

articles.title: WORKFLOW DRIVING 23% OF B2B PIPELINE
articles.summary: Bolt.new uncovered 23% of net new B2B pipeline, driven by Freckle.io. They enriched personal email sign-ups from high-potential companies. The workflow imported sign-ups from Snowflake, filtered personal and work emails, enriched contact and company data, scored and prioritized Tier 1 leads, and pushed them to HubSpot with LinkedIn engagement steps. In the first 4 weeks, this generated $1.7M in pipeline, including Fortune 500 deals, and the process has now been expanded to all demo requests.

articles.title: PROMPT LIKE A MARKETER
articles.summary: This guide gives a structured way to craft AI prompts by focusing on signal context, roles, jobs, formats, and tone. It shows how to use AI across key marketing tasks, offers reusable templates tied to real GTM scenarios, and helps teams turn buyer signals into clear outputs. It aims to sharpen messaging and support smarter use of AI while keeping human judgment central.

articles.title: AI EMAILS ME CONTENT IDEAS EVERY MORNING
articles.summary: This article explains how to set up a system where ChatGPT sends a daily email with categorized content ideas. The prompt pulls insights from past chats and organizes them into buckets for newsletters, LinkedIn, and email. Anyone can replicate the method by enabling ChatGPT schedules and applying a structured prompt. This reduces context switching and protects ideation from being lost in unstructured notes.

articles.title: HOW TO DO USER RESEARCH WITHOUT USERS: THE SYNTHETIC USER GUIDE
articles.summary: AI-generated personas give early-stage teams a fast way to clarify who they are building for and what those users care about. Tools like ChatGPT can create realistic, behavior-driven personas that reflect motivations, fears, workflows, and decision triggers without long research cycles. These personas help teams pressure test ideas, refine messaging, simulate interviews, and uncover blind spots before talking to real users. The approach speeds up alignment and improves product decisions while still requiring real user validation later on.

articles.title: 7 ECOMMERCE BLOG EXAMPLES
articles.summary: Ecommerce blogs that win in AI search and convert effectively combine educational content with smart product integration. Garmin ranks in the top AI visibility charts by creating detailed, structured articles that answer user questions while subtly linking to products. Petlibro balances top-of-funnel education with transactional keywords, creating posts cited by AI tools. Thinx builds authority through mission-aligned content on menstrual health and earns high rankings with concise, helpful posts.

articles.title: ANTHROPIC INTRODUCES CHEAPER, MORE POWERFUL, MORE EFFICIENT OPUS 4.5 MODEL
articles.summary: The update addresses previous limitations where long chats ended abruptly by introducing behind-the-scenes summarization that retains key context.

articles.title: THE SOCIAL-MEDIA PLATFORM THAT MAKES YOU TELL THE TRUTH
articles.summary: Strava turned fitness tracking into a social competition for over 150 million users.
